title: Human State Interface (HSI) 1.3
$comment: HSI 1.3 canonical schema. Adds the 5-axis canonical domain set (RFC-HSI-0010), per-modality fidelity tiers on sources (RFC-HSI-0011), and the categorical reading shape. `source_tier` is replaced by `tiers` on `meta.provenance.sources[*]`; readings and embeddings derive their effective per-modality tier by resolving evidence_source_ids. Some reference-integrity constraints MUST be enforced out-of-band for HSI-VALIDATE-STRICT compliance.

observed_at_utc.$comment: Event-time: the human state time (typically aligns with the latest window end).

computed_at_utc.$comment: Processing-time: when this payload was produced. Must be >= observed_at_utc (enforced out-of-band unless validator supports format comparisons).

windows.$comment: Map keys are the authoritative window IDs. axis_reading.window_ids and embedding.window_ids SHOULD reference these keys.

meta.$comment: meta may include provenance and other producer-defined keys. meta.provenance is the canonical place for source tracking in this version.

observable_modality.$comment: RFC-HSI-0010 §6 observable modality set. The fourth canonical modality `multimodal` is a fusion outcome and is NOT a valid observable; it cannot appear in modalities_used or as a tier key.

physio_tier_value.$comment: Physiological signal-fidelity tier per RFC-HSI-0011 §4.1. 1 = ground-truth (RR intervals, lab-grade ECG), 2 = vendor-derived high-fidelity, 3 = proxy time-series (≤1 Hz HR), 4 = snapshot/minimal.

kinematic_tier_value.$comment: Kinematic signal-fidelity tier per RFC-HSI-0011 §4.2. 1 = high-rate inertial (≥50 Hz multi-axis), 2 = event-based / coarse (steps, <25 Hz accel), 3 = sparse / vendor-aggregate (hourly bins, daily totals).

digital_tier_value.$comment: Digital-interaction signal-fidelity tier per RFC-HSI-0011 §4.3. 1 = rich (multi-kind events ≥30/min over ≥120 s), 2 = moderate (≥10/min over ≥60 s), 3 = sparse (<60 s windows or <10 events/min).

tiers.$comment: Per-modality fidelity tier set declared on a source in meta.provenance.sources (RFC-HSI-0011 §6). Lives at the source only; readings and embeddings derive their effective per-modality tier by resolving evidence_source_ids and taking the most conservative (highest-numbered) value across cited sources for each modality. Absence of a key MUST be interpreted as 'this modality did not contribute,' NOT as the worst-numbered tier.

window.$comment: HSI-VALIDATE-STRICT: window.end_utc MUST be >= window.start_utc (out-of-band unless validator supports format comparisons).

direction.$comment: RFC-HSI-0010 §7. `lower_is_more` replaces 1.2's `higher_is_less` (semantic clarification, not behavioral). `categorical` (new in 1.3) marks readings that carry a discrete class label rather than a scalar score; gated by §8.

inference_mode.$comment: probabilistic_model: learned/ensemble. deterministic_rule: formulas/rulepacks. external_provider: third-party or OS facts. composite: merged multi-source. Orthogonal to modality (encoded by axis-domain key, RFC-HSI-0010 §5.3).

name.$comment: Lower-snake-case axis member name. RFC-HSI-0010 §4.1–§4.5 list canonical members per domain; unknown names within a known domain MUST be tolerated (RFC-HSI-0008 §6.5).

score.$comment: score MAY be null when a producer cannot compute a reading; consumers MUST NOT treat null as zero. When direction == categorical, score MUST be null (see if/then below).

model_id.$comment: URI-shaped identifier (RFC 3986 scheme + path). Reserved schemes: 'model://', 'rulepack://', 'provider://'.

items.$comment: entries SHOULD reference keys in meta.provenance.sources. Resolve per-modality fidelity via each cited source's `tiers` field; the reading's effective tier per modality is the most conservative (highest-numbered) value across cited sources.

modalities_used.$comment: RFC-HSI-0010 §5.1. Required on readings in axes.cognitive[] and axes.affective[]; forbidden on readings in axes.physiological[] / axes.kinematic[] / axes.digital[] (the domain key already encodes modality). Placement is enforced at the axes-domain level (see /$defs/axes_domain_*) plus HSI-VALIDATE-STRICT.

label.$comment: Categorical class label. Required when direction == categorical; forbidden otherwise. MUST appear in `categories` (HSI-VALIDATE-STRICT).

categories.$comment: Full set of possible categories the producer may emit for this axis member. Required when direction == categorical; forbidden otherwise.

allOf.$comment: RFC-HSI-0010 §8: discriminated union on direction == categorical. When categorical, label/categories required and score MUST be null. When not categorical, label and categories MUST be absent.
RFC-HSI-0010 §5.1: modalities_used is forbidden on readings in single-modality domains; the axis-domain key already encodes the modality.
RFC-HSI-0010 §5.1: modalities_used is required on every reading in multimodal domains (cognitive, affective).

axes.$comment: RFC-HSI-0010 §4: closed 5-domain set. Adding a domain requires an RFC and schema bump.

type.$comment: Role of the source — what kind of thing produced the signal. Orthogonal to `device_class` (physical form factor), `signals` (what is measured), and `transport` (how the bytes reach the producer).

tiers.$comment: RFC-HSI-0011 §6.2: per-modality fidelity tier set for this source. Inherited by readings and embeddings that cite this source via evidence_source_ids; resolution rule in RFC-HSI-0011 §6.3. Replaces the 1.2 `source_tier` integer field.

device_class.$comment: Physical form factor. Only meaningful when `type` is a hardware-bearing role (typically `sensor`). Use `other` for unusual form factors; open an RFC before adding new canonical values.

signals.$comment: Physical or logical signals this source produces. Free-form lowercase tokens; suggested canonical names: `hrv`, `rr`, `ecg`, `ppg`, `spo2`, `resp`, `accel`, `gyro`, `mag`, `temp`, `eda`, `gsr`, `eeg`, `emg`, `touch`, `audio`. Consumers SHOULD tolerate unknown entries.

transport.$comment: How data reaches the producer. `inproc` = same-process APIs. `network` = generic IP when no more specific transport applies.

vendor.$comment: Free-form vendor/manufacturer tag (e.g. `polar`, `whoop`, `garmin`, `apple`). PRIVACY: highest-entropy field in `source`; producers SHOULD omit `vendor` in privacy-sensitive contexts where payloads may be joined across subjects (RFC-HSI-0008 §7).

sources.$comment: Map keys are the authoritative source IDs. evidence_source_ids on axis_reading and embeddings SHOULD reference these keys.

items.$comment: entries SHOULD reference keys in meta.provenance.sources. Per-modality fidelity is resolved against cited sources' `tiers` field.
